---
title: "Quickstart"
jupyter: python3
---

This walkthrough mirrors the *ExPanDaR functions* vignette using the bundled `gapminder`
panel (country × year, 1952–2007: life expectancy, population and GDP per capita).

```{python}
import numpy as np
import expdpy as ex
from expdpy.data import load_gapminder

df = load_gapminder()
df["log_gdppercap"] = np.log(df["gdpPercap"])
df[["country", "continent", "year", "lifeExp", "pop", "gdpPercap"]].head()
```

## Treating outliers

Winsorize the numeric variables at the 1st/99th percentile:

```{python}
analysis = ex.treat_outliers(
    df[["lifeExp", "gdpPercap", "pop"]], percentile=0.01
).dropna()
analysis.describe().round(3)
```

## Descriptive statistics

```{python}
ex.prepare_descriptive_table(analysis).gt
```

## Correlations

Pearson correlations appear above, Spearman below the diagonal; significant cells are bold.

```{python}
ex.prepare_correlation_table(analysis).gt
```

```{python}
ex.prepare_correlation_graph(analysis).fig
```

## Extreme observations

```{python}
ex.prepare_ext_obs_table(
    df, n=5, cs_id=["country"], ts_id="year", var="gdpPercap"
).gt
```

## Time trends

```{python}
ex.prepare_trend_graph(df, ts_id="year", var=["lifeExp"]).fig
```

```{python}
ex.prepare_quantile_trend_graph(df, ts_id="year", var="lifeExp").fig
```

## By-group views

```{python}
ex.prepare_by_group_bar_graph(df, "continent", "lifeExp", np.nanmedian).fig
```

```{python}
ex.prepare_by_group_violin_graph(df, "continent", "lifeExp")
```

## Scatter plot with LOESS

The classic Preston curve — life expectancy against GDP per capita, sized by population and
colored by continent:

```{python}
ex.prepare_scatter_plot(df, x="gdpPercap", y="lifeExp", color="continent", size="pop", loess=1)
```

## Regression with fixed effects and clustered SEs

Regress life expectancy on (log) GDP per capita with country fixed effects and standard
errors clustered by country:

```{python}
res = ex.prepare_regression_table(
    df,
    dvs="lifeExp",
    idvs=["log_gdppercap"],
    feffects=["country"],
    clusters=["country"],
)
res.etable
```
